steps.title: Simplify the given expression
steps.content:
Start with the radical in the denominator: $\sqrt{y^{12}} = y^{6}$.
$$\frac{3x^{19}y^{7}}{5x^{3}\sqrt{y^{12}}} = \frac{3x^{19}y^{7}}{5x^{3}y^{6}}$$
Apply exponent rules (subtract exponents when dividing like bases):
$$\frac{3}{5} \cdot x^{19-3} \cdot y^{7-6} = \frac{3}{5}x^{16}y$$

steps.title: Test Option I against the simplified form
steps.content:
Option I is $\frac{x^{\frac{16}{3}}y^{\frac{7}{3}}}{3}$. Comparing term-by-term to $\frac{3}{5}x^{16}y$:
  • Coefficient: $\frac{1}{3}$ versus $\frac{3}{5}$ - mismatch
  • Power of $x$: $\frac{16}{3}$ versus 16 - mismatch
  • Power of $y$: $\frac{7}{3}$ versus 1 - mismatch
Option I does not match.

steps.title: Test Option II and identify the answer
steps.content:
Option II is $\frac{3x^{12}y^{2}}{5}$. Comparing to $\frac{3}{5}x^{16}y$:
  • Coefficient: $\frac{3}{5}$ (matches)
  • Power of $x$: 12 versus 16 - mismatch
  • Power of $y$: 2 versus 1 - mismatch
The exponents do not match, so Option II is not equivalent. Neither option equals the simplified expression, that matches the correct answer - D is correct.
